response: The internet’s origins trace to the 1960s U.S. Department of Defense project that produced ARPANET, first demonstrated in 1969, and its adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 laid the technical foundation for today’s network. Tim Berners‑Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (HTML, URLs, HTTP) and the 1993 release of the Mosaic browser made the web usable for the general public, sparking the dot‑com boom of the late‑1990s and later the rise of social media, mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone launch, and cloud computing pioneered by AWS in 2006. Today, over 5 billion people are online, and the internet underpins virtually all facets of modern life, from commerce to communication to entertainment.
